summary: FIX VERIFIED for iter10 critical bug: missing `import { computeBantuGrid } from '../lib/bantuGrid';` is now present at line 5 of /app/frontend/src/audio/engine.js. End-to-end smoke test confirms PLAY+Bantu Swing no longer throws ReferenceError. Light regression sweep of 10 menus, Disk Usage modal, System Usage modal, and View→Waveforms→Rectified all pass.

fix_verification.scenario: Add MIDI track via sidebar [data-testid=add-midi-track] → toggle [data-testid=bantu-swing-toggle] ON → press PLAY
fix_verification.expected: PLAY label switches to STOP, status bar shows 'Playing all tracks', no runtime error overlay
fix_verification.actual: PLAY label switched to STOP, status bar shows 'Playing all tracks', playhead animated to 2.55s, Swing 70% badge visible, NO ReferenceError / NO 'Uncaught runtime error' overlay
fix_verification.result: PASS - the previous ReferenceError for computeBantuGrid is gone

swing_off_playback.scenario: Toggle Bantu Swing OFF then press PLAY
swing_off_playback.result: PASS - STOP button rendered, no runtime error

regression_checks.menus_opened: File, Edit, Track, Event, AudioSuite, Tools, View, Window, Setup, Help - all 10 opened cleanly with no runtime errors
regression_checks.disk_usage_modal: PASS - opens via Window menu, displays 5 simulated volumes (Macintosh HD, Sessions SSD, Samples RAID, Backup HDD, RIBA Cloud) with size/used/free/% and 48kHz 24-bit track-minutes column
regression_checks.system_usage_modal: PASS - opens via Window menu, shows CPU Total (16%), 24-thread Core Activity, Disk Activity (22%), Memory RAM (36%), Buffer Underruns (0), Voices Active (4), Session uptime
regression_checks.view_waveforms_rectified: PASS - status bar updated to 'Waveform mode: Rectified'

issues: React hydration warning: <span> rendered inside <option> in TrackRow line 71 (Vite dev-overlay instrumentation related). Not a runtime crash, but produces a console warning. Likely benign x-source-editable injection by dev tooling.

critical_code_review_comments: Fix is minimal and correct: a single ES module import at the top of engine.js resolves the ReferenceError. No additional changes needed.
Consider adding an ESLint rule (no-undef / import/no-unresolved) to catch future missing imports at lint time and prevent reintroduction.
engine.js is 653 lines - approaching the recommended split threshold (>700). Future refactor could extract swing/grid scheduling into a separate module.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter10 critical ReferenceError fixed by adding import on line 5 of engine.js. Confirmed via PLAY+Swing flow + 10-menu regression sweep + Disk/System Usage modals + View→Waveforms→Rectified. Only residual is a benign hydration warning in TrackRow option/span (dev-tool injected attributes). Backend pytest (42/42 in iter10) was not re-run as no backend code changed.
rca of the issue: Original RCA from iter10: engine.js called computeBantuGrid() without importing it from ../lib/bantuGrid → ReferenceError on PLAY when Swing was enabled. Fix: added `import { computeBantuGrid } from '../lib/bantuGrid';` at engine.js:5. Verified by clicking PLAY with Swing ON and observing playback start + status bar update + absence of error overlay.
